title: Senja + Vesterålen
tagline: Coastal loop via Gryllefjord→Andenes. Slow Senja + a whale/Bleik day; return is compressed so the islands get the hours.
anchors: Ferry Gryllefjord → Andenes (Tue 1 Sep) — queue overnight if needed, no booking
Fri 4 Sep: Lyngen east shore → Svensby ferry → Tromsø buffer night

overnight.name: Near Brensholmen ferry quay
overnight.alt_campsite: Utsikten Bobil Camping (near Brensholmen / Sommarøy road)

notes.text: Late pickup — keep the day short.
Stock groceries + water before leaving Tromsø.
Sleep close to the quay so Sunday morning ferry is relaxed.

optional.notes: Extends the Grøtfjord stop — white sand + turquoise water; good drone light if calm

scenarios.when: Prefer a bookable / facilities campsite instead of wild overnight
scenarios.summary: Sleep Utsikten Bobil Camping (showers/power) near Brensholmen — still an easy morning run to the ferry quay.

overnight.name: Utsikten Bobil Camping
overnight.price: 300 NOK / night pitch for the van (Park4Night / guest reports; VIPPS or cash on-site)

waypoints.name: Overnight Utsikten Bobil Camping
waypoints.price: 300 NOK / night pitch for the van (Park4Night / guest reports; VIPPS or cash on-site)

notes.text: Late pickup — keep the day short.
Stock groceries + water before leaving Tromsø.
Utsikten Bobil Camping is the named motorhome pitch north of the quay (VIPPS / on-site pay; no online booking). ~5–10 min drive to Brensholmen in the morning.

overnight.name: Camp Mefjord, Mefjordvær
overnight.alt_scenic: Pull-off above Mefjordvær if campsite full
overnight.price: ≈325–440 NOK / night pitch for the van (2026 reports; confirm on booking)

ferry.price: 247 NOK / van ≤6 m (our 5.99 m; 2026; AutoPASS/FerryPay; passengers free)

notes.text: Sunday 10:15 sailing — first useful departure.
Scenic Route starts here — stop often; empty the afternoon.
Aim for Tungeneset in good evening light (Devil’s Teeth).

optional.notes: Short boardwalk / shore path to the light at the north tip — easy village linger after the ferry; park before the bridge if the island is tight.
Best light late afternoon/evening — protect this if weather is clear; skip kayak if you do this
Calm sea only — pick kayak OR evening Tungeneset, not both
Easy ~30–45 min hill from the football-field / trail parking — fjord and village panorama; good evening walk from Camp Mefjord.

scenarios.summary: Take the next Sunday sailing (12:15). Senja afternoon compresses — keep Tungeneset light if you can.

replace.title: Late ferry to Senja → northern coast (compressed)

ferry.season_note: Missed 10:15 — next useful Sunday departure from Brensholmen is 12:15 (~35 min).

notes.text: Sunday backup after 10:15 is 12:15 (then 17:00).
Skip kayak; protect a shorter Tungeneset stop if light remains.
Still sleep Mefjordvær — Day 3 is unchanged.

optional.notes: Short version — only if you arrive with daylight

scenarios.when: Prefer wild overnight instead of Camp Mefjord
scenarios.summary: Sleep a quiet pull-off in/above Mefjordvær village (allemannsretten) if the campsite is full or you skip facilities.

notes.text: Sunday 10:15 sailing — first useful departure.
Scenic Route starts here — stop often; empty the afternoon.
Aim for Tungeneset in good evening light (Devil’s Teeth).
Wild overnight near Mefjordvær — stay ≥150 m from houses, leave no trace; Camp Mefjord remains the facilities fallback.

days.title: Slow Senja Scenic Route → Gryllefjord queue

overnight.name: Gryllefjord ferry queue / harbour
overnight.alt_campsite: Senja Fjordcamp in Torsken (~8 km from Gryllefjord quay)

notes.text: Linger on the Scenic Route spine — Bergsbotn, Ersfjordstranda, then Hamn. Bøvær is an optional fishing-village beach detour.
Hamn is the calm-water hub (kayak/SUP/sauna) before the queue.
Arrive Gryllefjord late afternoon / early evening and join the camper queue for 11:00.
Peak options at Fjordgård: Hesten (shorter view of Segla) or Segla summit — pick one; skip both if the Gryllefjord queue is tight.

optional.notes: NTV lists Bøvær as a detour off the Scenic Route — white sand / fishing village; skip if short on time for the Gryllefjord queue
Classic Senja peak (~3–4 h RT; turn around at Sommardalen ~2 h for a shorter view) — clearer weather preferred; trail from Berg kirke / Skaland.
Calm-water session at Hamn — pick kayak OR sauna if short on time
Paid — warm Hamn soak; pick kayak OR sauna if short on time
Best classic Segla photo angle — steep near the top; clear weather only. Pick Hesten OR Segla, not both.
Iconic Senja peak (~3.5 h / steep ridge) — harder than Hesten; clear dry weather only. Pick Segla OR Hesten, not both.
Steep ~2–2.5 h above Hamn with coast views — skip if wet/slippery on the final scramble; chain section near the top.

scenarios.when: Prefer a campsite instead of sleeping in the Gryllefjord ferry queue
scenarios.summary: Sleep Senja Fjordcamp (Torsken) for showers/power; drive ~10 min back to the quay for the 11:00 sailing.

replace.title: Slow Senja Scenic Route → Senja Fjordcamp

overnight.name: Senja Fjordcamp (Torsken)
overnight.price: 400 NOK / night for 2 adults (incl. electricity + showers; bobilplassen.no 2026 / NorCamp)

waypoints.name: Overnight Senja Fjordcamp (Torsken)
waypoints.price: 400 NOK / night for 2 adults (incl. electricity + showers; bobilplassen.no 2026 / NorCamp)

notes.text: Linger on the Scenic Route spine — Bergsbotn, Ersfjordstranda, then Hamn. Bøvær is an optional fishing-village beach detour.
Hamn is the calm-water hub (kayak/SUP/sauna) before overnight.
Still check the Gryllefjord quay / queue etiquette in the evening — then sleep at Senja Fjordcamp in Torsken (~8 km).
Showers and power at Fjordcamp; book via Senja by Heart if you can. Leave early for the 11:00 ferry.
Peak options at Fjordgård: Hesten (shorter view of Segla) or Segla summit — pick one; skip both if the Gryllefjord queue is tight.

overnight.name: Midnattsol Camping, Bleik
overnight.alt_campsite: Andenes Camping (north of Bleik — small backtrack if Midnattsol is full)
overnight.alt_scenic: Bleik beach parking overnight if legal/quiet
overnight.price: 445 NOK / night for 2 adults (410 camper+1+electricity + 35 extra adult)

ferry.season_note: From 24 Aug–27 Sep 2026: only 11:00 and 19:00 from Gryllefjord. No vehicle booking — first come, first served. Arrive 2–3h early with a camper.
ferry.price: 925 NOK van ≤6 m (driver incl.) + 100 NOK passenger 30–66 → ≈1025 NOK for 2 adults (2026 from 15 Jun; FerryPay/AutoPASS)

optional.notes: Book ahead if possible; pick boat OR museum/lighthouse stack, not everything
optional.price: 2×1690 NOK ≈ 3380 NOK for 2 adults (RIB or boat; incl. exhibition)

optional.notes: Sperm-whale exhibition by the harbour — good if you skip or shorten the boat, or want context before sailing.
Tall red lighthouse at the tip — skip in strong wind; pairs well with a short harbour walk.
Visitor centre between Andenes and Bleik — northern lights / rocket exhibits; check opening hours.
Golden-hour evening on white sand; drone only if wind + bird rules allow

notes.text: Keep the afternoon for whale safari; keep evening for Bleik.
Do not stack museum + lighthouse + Spaceship on top of a full whale safari — pick one indoor/harbour block.
Book whale safari the evening before or early morning if seats look good.
Sleep Midnattsol Camping on Bleik beach — cook dinner after the walk; Day 5 continues south on the Scenic Route toward Bjørnskinn (no return to Andenes).

scenarios.summary: Take the 19:00 sailing. Whale moves to Wednesday morning; Andøya + return compress.

ferry.season_note: Missed the 11:00 — taking the 19:00 Gryllefjord→Andenes (~100 min). Stay near the quay; do not day-trip Senja.

notes.text: Stay near the quay; do not day-trip Senja and risk missing 19:00.
On arrival: groceries, campsite, early night — whale is tomorrow.

optional.notes: No safari tonight — protect Wednesday morning

scenarios.banner: Ferry fail — wait for 19:00, sleep Andenes late

ripple.banner: Ferry fail — whale morning, then race south

replace.title: Whale morning → Bleik → hard push toward Nyksund/Myre

notes.text: Book the earliest whale departure you can.
Skip Måtinden; Bleik is a short stop only.

At Børvågen lies Andøya Spaceport. The road here (FV 7698 / Nordmelaveien) can be closed for up to 8 hours due to operational activities — check before driving: https://andoyaspace.no/spaceport/nyttig-info-spaceport/
Sleep Nyksund if you arrive before dusk, else Myre/Stø campsite.

optional.notes: Do this first — everything else yields
optional.price: 2×1690 NOK ≈ 3380 NOK for 2 adults (RIB or boat; incl. exhibition)

ripple.banner: Ferry fail — even more compressed mainland hop

notes.text: Leave at first light. Sortland fuel/food only.
Accept a long van day — islands already got the ferry delay tax.

scenarios.when: Prefer wild overnight instead of Midnattsol Camping
scenarios.summary: Sleep Bleik beach parking if legal/quiet — no showers/power; still on tomorrow’s Andøya spine.

notes.text: Keep the afternoon for whale safari; keep evening for Bleik.
Do not stack museum + lighthouse + Spaceship on top of a full whale safari — pick one harbour block.
Book whale safari the evening before or early morning if seats look good.
Wild overnight at Bleik beach parking only if signs allow / it stays quiet — Midnattsol Camping is the facilities default.

ripple.banner: Wild alt — start from Bleik beach

overnight.name: Nyksund harbour / village edge
overnight.alt_campsite: Stø Bobilcamp (near Nyksund); Oppmyre Camping near Myre as backup

notes.text: Follow Nasjonal turistveg Andøya (https://www.nasjonaleturistveger.no/en/routes/andoya/) — official rest areas Kleivodden + Bukkekjerka pin the outer-coast road.
Kleivodden is the official rest area just north of Bleik (short jog toward Andenes, then continue south).
Måtinden is the island’s signature short mountain walk (turn back early if tired).

At Børvågen lies Andøya Spaceport. The road here (FV 7698 / Nordmelaveien) can be closed for up to 8 hours due to operational activities — check before driving: https://andoyaspace.no/spaceport/nyttig-info-spaceport/
Bukkekjerka is the southern official rest area (Sami sacrificial site / sea views) — short stop only.
Bjørnskinn is the south end of the Scenic Route; leave Andøya via Risøyhamn toward Nyksund.
Arrive Nyksund with afternoon left to linger — cook with harbour view.

optional.notes: Clear-weather walk — turn around at the plateau if legs/time say so; skip Stø if you do the full hike · Røyken is the shorter Andøya alternative if legs/time are short
Ocean-view heated pools + beach sauna on the Andøya Scenic Route — book ahead; evening slots sell out.
Easier Andøya peak (~1.5–2 h) with Bleikstranda views — good if you skip or shorten Måtinden; trail from the Space Center gravel road.
Sea eagles / fishing village — only if you skipped a long Måtinden; leave the Scenic Route after Bjørnskinn
Village linger — highlight of the evening

scenarios.when: Prefer a campsite instead of wild overnight at Nyksund
scenarios.summary: Visit Nyksund, then sleep Stø Bobilcamp (~5 min north) for facilities.

Kleivodden is the official rest area just north of Bleik (short jog toward Andenes, then continue south).
Måtinden is the island’s signature short mountain walk (turn back early if tired).

At Børvågen lies Andøya Spaceport. The road here (FV 7698 / Nordmelaveien) can be closed for up to 8 hours due to operational activities — check before driving: https://andoyaspace.no/spaceport/nyttig-info-spaceport/
Bukkekjerka is the southern official rest area (Sami sacrificial site / sea views) — short stop only.
Bjørnskinn is the south end of the Scenic Route; leave Andøya via Risøyhamn toward Nyksund / Stø.
Linger in Nyksund village, then sleep Stø Bobilcamp (motorhome pitches; first-come — Oppmyre Camping near Myre if Stø is full).

ripple.banner: Campsite alt — start from Stø Bobilcamp

notes.text: Transit day by design — hours were spent on Senja/Andøya instead.
Fuel + groceries in Sortland; then Rv85 → E10 → E6 to Bardu — optional short corridor photo stops only (Gullesfjord, Tjeldsund Bridge, Bjerkvik).
Sleep on the E6 corridor so Friday can take the Lyngen east-shore scenic approach into Tromsø.

overnight.name: Bardu Camping, Setermoen
overnight.alt_scenic: E6 pull-off only if you already have water/showers

notes.text: Transit day by design — hours were spent on Senja/Andøya instead.
Fuel + groceries in Sortland; then Rv85 → E10 → E6 to Bardu — optional short corridor photo stops at Gullesfjord, Tjeldsund Bridge, and Bjerkvik.
Sleep on the E6 corridor so Friday can take the Lyngen east-shore scenic approach into Tromsø.

optional.notes: First real wow after Sortland — fjord + peaks at the Rv85/E10 junction (Møysalen edge); 15–20 min stretch only.
Classic mainland-gateway view over Tjeldsundet — park safely and keep it short (10–15 min).
E10/E6 junction fjord views before the inland push to Setermoen — good last stretch stop (15–20 min).

optional.activity: Hurtigruten Museum (only if weather is foul)
optional.notes: Detour — skip unless you need an indoor break; adds time

scenarios.when: Prefer wild overnight instead of Bardu Camping
scenarios.summary: E6 corridor pull-off near Setermoen only if showers/water are already done — no facilities.

Wild E6 pull-off near Setermoen — only if you already have water/showers; Bardu Camping is the facilities default.

days.title: Lyngen east shore → Svensby ferry → Tromsø buffer

overnight.name: Tromsø Lodge & Camping (Tromsdalen)
overnight.alt_scenic: Only if showers already done — stay within ~30 min of depot
overnight.price: 550 NOK / night pitch for the van (low season from 1 Sep 2026; confirm if person fees)

ferry.note: Frequent daytime sailings (~hourly). No booking needed for campers usually. Deck views of the Lyngen Alps across Ullsfjorden.
ferry.price: 104 NOK / van ≤6 m (our 5.99 m; 2026; AutoPASS/FerryPay; passengers free)

notes.text: After Målselvfossen, stay on the E6 past Nordkjosbotn, then at Oteren turn onto Fv868 north along Lyngenfjord (Alps across the water; pull-offs are sparse). Overview: https://nordnorge.com/en/artikkel/take-a-driving-tour-around-the-lyngen-alps/
Lyngseidet is a short village linger (church / café) — then cross the peninsula to Svensby for the ferry.
Aim for a mid-afternoon Svensby → Breivikeidet sailing; still reach Tromsø Lodge with evening left for van service.
Empty grey water / refill / showers / fuel tonight — buffer before 11:30 return.

optional.notes: Worth 45 minutes if the river is lively — otherwise drive past
West-shore glacier approach between Lyngseidet and Svensby — short marked walk / viewpoint only (no glacier travel); skip if ferry timing is tight.
Short dead-end north of Lyngseidet — glacier/peak angles; skip if ferry timing is tight
Easy linger before the Svensby ferry
Water / dump / showers / fuel before buffer night
Easy Tromsø classic from the buffer night — views over the city and islands; last rides earlier off-peak — check timetable.
optional.price: 2×495 NOK = 990 NOK for 2 adults (return; official tariff)

scenarios.summary: Wait ~+1 h. Still reach Tromsø buffer night — do van service on arrival.

replace.title: Lyngen east shore → late Svensby ferry → Tromsø buffer

ferry.season_note: Missed Svensby → Breivikeidet — next daytime sailing ~+1 h.

notes.text: Stay at Svensby quay — crossings are frequent.
Skip Koppangen if you already missed a sailing; buffer night near depot still works.
Service the van after arrival.

optional.notes: Do this after the late ferry — still before overnight

scenarios.when: Prefer wild overnight instead of Tromsø Lodge & Camping
scenarios.summary: Sleep Telegrafbukta (south Tromsø) within ~20 min of the depot — only after showers/service elsewhere.

Lyngseidet is a short village linger (church / café) — then cross the peninsula to Svensby for the ferry.

Wild overnight at Telegrafbukta — stay quiet, follow local signs; do van service before sleeping. Tromsø Lodge remains the facilities default.
Return appointment 11:30 — leave overnight with ~30 min buffer.
